info.title: DOIT Agent API - Extended
info.description: Complete API for Azure AI Agent with read and write operations for MongoDB data, Document Intelligence (Azure DI + GPT-4o-mini), and Data Visualization (Plotly/Seaborn). IMPORTANT: Every message includes a [Context: {...}] section with user_email field. When calling write operations (createTask, assignTask, etc.), ALWAYS extract user_email from the Context and use it as the requesting_user parameter. Example: If Context shows 'user_email': 'testuser@gmail.com', then use requesting_user: 'testuser@gmail.com' in your API calls.

servers.description: Public tunnel to development server via ngrok

get.description: ALWAYS call this FIRST to get project list with internal project_id values. Each project has a 'name' field (human-readable) and 'project_id' field (internal MongoDB ID). When user mentions a project name, use this endpoint to find and extract the project_id field.
Retrieve tasks from MongoDB with optional filters for project, status, priority, and assignee.
Retrieve all users (excluding sensitive information like passwords and tokens)
Retrieve sprints with optional filters for project and status
Retrieve aggregated statistics including total counts, task distributions by status and priority, users by role, and projects by status

get.summary: Get specific project details by internal ID
get.description: Get details for a specific project using its internal project_id (MongoDB ObjectId). NEVER use project name here.
Check if the agent API and database connection are healthy

post.description: Create a task with automatic assignee resolution and RBAC validation. CRITICAL: Extract user_email from [Context] and use as requesting_user.

403.description: Only Admin users can delete tasks
Only Admin users can approve tasks
Only Admin users can create projects
Only Admin users can update projects
Only Admin users can delete projects
Only Admin users can add members
Only Admin users can create sprints

post.summary: Generate structured report and return a real download URL
post.description: Generate a structured report from analysis output and return a downloadable file URL in the API response. Prefer download_url/download_markdown for user-facing links; use pdf_base64 only as fallback. For email or Logic App handoff, set omit_pdf_base64=true and pass attachment_url/download_url to avoid 502 timeout due to large payloads. Do NOT assume SharePoint storage. CRITICAL: extract user_email from [Context] and pass it as requesting_user (query or body).

parameters.name: requesting_user
parameters.description: Optional duplicate of body.requesting_user for connector compatibility

omit_pdf_base64.description: Set true when sending report through mail/Logic App to keep payload small and avoid timeout/502

download_url.description: Preferred absolute URL for clickable download

download_markdown.description: Ready-to-use markdown link text for chat UIs

pdf_base64_included.description: Indicates whether pdf_base64 is included in this response

email_handoff.description: Compact payload for Mail_Agent / Logic App

pdf_base64.description: Base64-encoded PDF bytes for direct download

post.summary: Analyze a document or URL with Azure Document Intelligence + GPT-4o-mini
post.description: PRIMARY document analysis endpoint. Upload a file (PDF, DOCX, XLSX, CSV, PPTX, TXT, images) OR pass a public URL. Parser priority: (1) Azure Document Intelligence for PDF/DOCX/images — extracts text, tables, key-value pairs via OCR; (2) Docling for PPTX/TXT/URLs; (3) Pandas for CSV/XLSX. GPT-4o-mini then generates KPIs, business insights, top/bottom performers, recommendations, and executive summary. Use this when the user uploads a document, asks to analyze a file, or wants insights from a URL. Returns a full InsightReport JSON.

file.description: Document file to analyze. Supported: PDF, DOCX, DOC, PPTX, PPT, TXT, CSV, XLSX, XLS, JPEG, JPG, PNG, TIFF, BMP. Use this OR url, not both.

url.description: Publicly accessible URL of a document or webpage to analyze. Use this OR file, not both. Examples: annual report URL, news article, public PDF link.

question.description: Optional business question to focus the analysis. Leave empty for comprehensive analysis. Examples: 'What are the top revenue risks?', 'Who are the top 5 performers?', 'What is the sales trend?'

200.description: InsightReport JSON with full AI analysis

doc_name.description: Name of the analyzed document or URL

summary.description: 3-4 sentence executive summary with specific numbers and key patterns

parser_used.description: Parser that extracted the document: 'Azure Document Intelligence', 'Docling', or 'pandas'

kpis.description: 6-8 Key Performance Indicators extracted from the document

insights.description: 6-8 business insights with specific data references

body.description: 3-4 sentences with specific names, numbers, and percentages from the data

top_performers.description: Top 5 individuals/entities ranked by primary metric (populated when names exist in data)

score_distribution.description: Count of items in each performance band (populated for scored datasets)

example.question: What are the top revenue risks?
example.summary: Q1 revenue reached $4.2M, 12% below target. Three regions underperformed: APAC (-23%), EMEA (-8%), and LATAM (-15%). Top performer was John Smith at $820K closed deals.

insights.title: APAC Region Underperformance
insights.body: APAC missed targets by 23% ($340K shortfall). Key contributors: pipeline delays in Singapore (3 deals, $180K) and team turnover in Sydney (2 senior reps departed Q4).

recommendations: Re-assign Singapore pipeline deals to top performer John Smith by April 1 to recover $180K at risk.
Hire 2 replacement reps in Sydney within 30 days to prevent further Q2 revenue gap.

top_performers.name: John Smith
top_performers.highlights: Highest deal closure rate (94%), 3 enterprise deals this quarter

400.description: No file or URL provided, or unsupported file type

500.description: Azure DI or GPT processing failed

post.summary: Export an InsightReport as a branded dark-themed PDF
post.description: Convert a previously generated InsightReport JSON (from analyzeDocument) into a downloadable dark-branded PDF built with ReportLab. The PDF includes: executive summary, KPI cards, score distribution, top/bottom performer tables, business insights, and strategic recommendations. Use this when user asks to download, export, or save the analysis as a PDF.

schema.description: Full InsightReport object returned from analyzeDocument. Pass it back as-is.

get.description: Returns status of Azure Document Intelligence, Azure OpenAI GPT, and parser availability. Use this before analyzeDocument to verify all services are configured.

post.summary: Upload a CSV or Excel dataset for visualization
post.description: Upload a CSV or XLSX/XLS file. The backend parses it with Pandas, stores it in MongoDB, and returns metadata including column names, data types, row count, and a preview. ALWAYS call this first before analyzeDataset or visualizeDataset. Returns a dataset_id needed for subsequent calls. IMPORTANT: pass requesting_user from Context.user_email.

parameters.name: requesting_user
parameters.description: Email from Context.user_email for user-scoped dataset ownership

file.description: CSV or Excel file (CSV, XLSX, XLS). Max recommended size: 10MB.

dataset_id.description: MongoDB ID for this dataset — use in analyzeDataset and visualizeDataset

dtypes.description: Column name → data type mapping e.g. {'Sales': 'float64', 'Region': 'object'}

preview.description: First 5 rows as array of objects

numeric_columns.description: Columns with numeric data — valid options for x/y in visualizeDataset

categorical_columns.description: Columns with text/categorical data — valid options for color/group in visualizeDataset

400.description: Invalid file type. Only CSV, XLSX, XLS accepted.

get.summary: List all uploaded datasets for the current user
get.description: Returns all datasets previously uploaded by the user, with metadata (filename, rows, columns, upload date). Use this to let the user pick a dataset without re-uploading. IMPORTANT: pass requesting_user from Context.user_email.

parameters.name: requesting_user
parameters.description: Email from Context.user_email for user-scoped dataset listing

post.summary: Run statistical analysis on an uploaded dataset
post.description: Perform deep statistical analysis on a dataset using Pandas. Returns: descriptive statistics (mean, std, min, max, quartiles) for all numeric columns, correlation matrix between numeric columns, value distributions for categorical columns, and missing value counts. Use this to understand a dataset before choosing visualization type and axes. IMPORTANT: pass requesting_user from Context.user_email.

parameters.name: requesting_user
parameters.description: Email from Context.user_email for user-scoped dataset access

descriptive_stats.description: Per-column stats: count, mean, std, min, 25%, 50%, 75%, max

correlation_matrix.description: Pearson correlation between all numeric column pairs (-1 to 1)

value_distributions.description: For categorical columns: value counts and percentages

suggested_charts.description: Agent-recommended chart types based on data shape

post.summary: Generate an interactive Plotly or static Seaborn chart from a dataset
post.description: Create a chart from an uploaded dataset. Plotly charts are interactive HTML (hover, zoom, legend toggle) stored in MongoDB. Seaborn/Matplotlib charts are static PNG base64. IMPORTANT: x_column and y_column must be actual column names from the dataset (get them from uploadDataset or listDatasets). Returns a viz_id to retrieve the chart via getVisualization. IMPORTANT: pass requesting_user from Context.user_email.

parameters.name: requesting_user
parameters.description: Email from Context.user_email for user-scoped dataset access

chart_type.description: Chart type to generate. scatter/line/bar for trend and comparison. histogram/box/violin for distribution. heatmap for correlations. pie for proportions.

x_column.description: Column name for X axis. Must be an actual column name from the dataset.

y_column.description: Column name for Y axis (numeric recommended). Must be an actual column name. Not required for histogram or pie.

color_column.description: Optional categorical column to color-code data points (e.g. 'Region', 'Category')

title.description: Chart title. If omitted, auto-generated from column names.

renderer.description: Rendering engine. plotly = interactive HTML. seaborn/matplotlib = static PNG.

aggregation.description: Optional aggregation for bar/line charts when multiple rows share the same x value.

viz_id.description: Visualization ID — use in getVisualization or downloadVisualization

preview_url.description: Direct URL to view/download the chart

400.description: Invalid column names or chart configuration

get.summary: List all visualizations created by the current user
get.description: Returns all charts previously generated by the user, with metadata (chart type, title, dataset, creation date). Use this to let the user browse and re-access past visualizations. IMPORTANT: pass requesting_user from Context.user_email.

parameters.name: requesting_user
parameters.description: Email from Context.user_email for user-scoped visualization listing

get.summary: Download or view a generated chart
get.description: Retrieve a previously generated chart by viz_id. Plotly charts return as interactive HTML (open in browser). Seaborn/Matplotlib charts return as PNG image. Use format query param to force PNG or HTML.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Output format. html for interactive Plotly chart. png for static image.

delete.summary: Delete a dataset and all its associated visualizations
delete.description: Permanently delete a dataset from MongoDB along with all charts generated from it. Use when the user wants to clean up or remove uploaded data. IMPORTANT: pass requesting_user from Context.user_email.

parameters.name: requesting_user
parameters.description: Email from Context.user_email for user-scoped dataset deletion

200.description: Dataset and associated visualizations deleted successfully

403.description: Not authorized to delete this dataset

AgentAPIKey.name: api_key
AgentAPIKey.description: Agent service authentication key (query parameter for Azure AI compatibility)

InsightReport.description: Full document analysis result from analyzeDocument
